name: Singapore
cultural_context: Multicultural city-state with strong emphasis on efficiency, pragmatism, and meritocracy. Kiasu (fear of losing out) culture drives consumer behavior.
local_brands: Grab, Shopee, FairPrice, DBS, Tiger Beer, Charles & Keith
festivals: Chinese New Year, Hari Raya, Deepavali, National Day (Aug 9)
values: Efficiency, family, education, food culture, pragmatism
humor_style: Self-deprecating, Singlish wordplay, relatable everyday situations
taboos: Avoid racial/religious insensitivity, don't mock government policies, be careful with 'four' (death association in Chinese culture)

name: Thailand
cultural_context: Land of Smiles with deep Buddhist influence. Respect for monarchy and elders is paramount. Thai consumers love fun, playful, and emotional content.
local_brands: LINE, Lazada, CP Group, Singha, PTT, SCB
festivals: Songkran (Apr), Loy Krathong (Nov), King's Birthday, Buddhist holidays
values: Sanuk (fun), respect for elders, Buddhism, face-saving, hospitality
humor_style: Slapstick, over-the-top, emotional storytelling, celebrity endorsements
taboos: Never disrespect the monarchy, avoid using Buddha images commercially, don't point feet at people/images, head is sacred

name: Malaysia
cultural_context: Multiracial society (Malay, Chinese, Indian) with strong Islamic influence. Halal compliance is critical for many products. Bahasa Rojak (mixed language) is common.
local_brands: Grab, Petronas, Shopee, AirAsia, Mamee, Bata
festivals: Hari Raya Aidilfitri, Chinese New Year, Deepavali, Merdeka Day (Aug 31)
values: Family, religious harmony, food culture, gotong-royong (community spirit)
humor_style: Bahasa Rojak wordplay, self-deprecating, food references, relatable family scenarios
taboos: Halal sensitivity, avoid pork/alcohol imagery for Muslim audience, respect for royalty, avoid racial stereotypes

name: Indonesia
cultural_context: World's largest Muslim-majority country with incredible diversity (300+ ethnic groups). Strong community values and growing digital economy.
local_brands: Gojek, Tokopedia, Indomie, BCA, Traveloka, Wardah
festivals: Eid al-Fitr (Lebaran), Independence Day (Aug 17), Ramadan, Nyepi (Bali)
values: Gotong royong (mutual aid), religious piety, family, nationalism, respect for elders
humor_style: Warm, community-oriented, relatable daily life, gentle humor, meme culture
taboos: Halal compliance critical, avoid left-hand imagery, respect religious symbols, be careful with LGBTQ+ content

name: China
cultural_context: World's largest consumer market with unique digital ecosystem (WeChat, Douyin, Xiaohongshu). Strong emphasis on collectivism, face culture, and rapid trend cycles. Consumers are brand-savvy and value quality, social proof, and KOL endorsements.
local_brands: Alibaba, Tencent, ByteDance, Xiaomi, Huawei, Nayuki, Heytea, Li-Ning, Anta
festivals: Chinese New Year (Spring Festival), Mid-Autumn Festival, Singles Day (11.11), Dragon Boat Festival, Qingming, National Day (Oct 1)
values: Family, harmony, education, face (mianzi), hard work, collective success, filial piety
humor_style: Wordplay and puns (especially homophones), self-deprecating humor, internet slang (e.g. YYDS, jianguo), meme culture, satirical content on Bilibili
taboos: Avoid political sensitivity (Taiwan, Tibet, Tiananmen), do not use the number 4 prominently (death association), avoid Japanese militarism references, respect CCP guidelines, no skull/skeleton imagery in ads, careful with religious content

name: Philippines
cultural_context: Strongly influenced by American and Spanish culture. Very social media active. Filipinos love storytelling, humor, and emotional content. Taglish (Tagalog-English mix) is the norm.
local_brands: Jollibee, Grab, Shopee, Globe, SM, Bench
festivals: Christmas (starts Sep!), Sinulog, Ati-Atihan, Independence Day (Jun 12)
values: Bayanihan (community help), family, faith, pakikisama (social harmony), humor in adversity
humor_style: Self-deprecating, puns in Taglish, meme-heavy, celebrity culture, emotional storytelling
taboos: Respect for religion (Catholic majority), avoid insensitive poverty references, don't mock Filipino English accent
